data.title: Bonk board member Mitchell Rudy acquires stock to close Q4
data.description:
• Bonk (BNKK) on Friday said Mitchell Rudy, a member of the company's Board of Directors, executed an open-market purchase of 13,142 shares of the company's common stock at the close of the fourth quarter.
  • BNKK +7.22% premarket to $2.97.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Scienjoy receives additional 180 days from Nasdaq to meet compliance rules
data.description:
• Scienjoy Holding (NASDAQ:SJ) stated on Friday that Nasdaq granted an additional 180-day extension on Jan. 7, 2026, to regain compliance with the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement under Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).
  • The company could face delisting if it fails to regain compliance by July 6, 2026, and may seek a review of any delisting determination by a Nasdaq Hearings Panel.
  • Shares -4.93%.

data.title: Housing starts fall more than expected in September
data.description: Housing starts slid 4.6% M/M to a 1.246M annual rate in September, compared with the 1.330M consensus and the prior month's 1.307M, according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Friday. This was before the government shutdown started Oct. 1.

data.title: flyExclusive prices of $15 million public offering
data.description:
• flyExclusive (FLYX) on Friday announced the pricing of its public offering of 2.26M shares of its common stock at a public offering price of $6.65 per share of common stock.
  • Lucid Capital Markets is acting as the sole book-running manager for the offering.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 12, 202.
  • FLYX -4.15% premarket to $6.93.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Delcath Systems expects FY revenue of $85.2 million
data.description:
• Delcath Systems (DCTH) said on Friday its total fourth-quarter revenue is expected to be approximately $20.7M vs. a consensus of $19.99M and full-year revenue of $85.2M vs. a consensus of $84.49M, respectively
  • HEPZATO KIT fourth quarter and full-year revenue expected to be approximately $19.0 million and $78.8 million, respectively
  • CHEMOSAT  fourth quarter and full-year revenue expected to be approximately $1.7 million and $6.4 million, respectively
  • The company said it repurchased 628,572 common shares for $6.0 million through December 31, 2025, under the approved $25.0 million Share Buyback Program.
  • As of December 31, 2025, the company had approximately $91.0 million of cash and short-term investments and no debt compared to cash and short-term investments of $88.9 million as of September 30, 2025
  • DCTH -0.97% premarket to $10.11.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ondas prices $1B registered direct offering, shares up
data.description:
Ondas Holdings (ONDS) on Friday announced the pricing of $1B registered direct offering to an institutional investor of 19M shares and, in lieu of common stock, pre-funded warrants to purchase up to 41.79M shares.

Shares were +4.35% pre-market to $14.62.

Each share and the accompanying common stock warrant is being sold together at a combined offering price of $16.45.

Each pre-funded warrant and accompanying common stock warrant is being sold together at a combined offering price of $16.4499.

Each common stock warrant will have an exercise price of $28.00 per share.

The offering is expected to close on or about January 12, 2026.

If the common stock warrants are fully exercised on a cash basis, Ondas has the potential to raise ~$3.4B in additional gross proceeds. 

Net proceeds are intended to be used for corporate development and strategic growth, including acquisitions, joint ventures, and investments.

data.title: Koppers CFO Jimmi Sue Smith to retire
data.description:
Koppers Holdings (KOP) on Friday announced the retirement of CFO Jimmi Sue Smith from the post, effective January 5, 2026.

Smith will continue to serve as treasurer, as well as in an advisory role, through February 28, 2026.

Chief Accounting Officer Bradley Pearce was elected by the board to serve in the additional post of interim CFO.

data.title: Stablecoin infrastructure company Rain valued at $1.95B in latest funding round
data.description:
Rain raised $250M in its series C funding round led by ICONIQ, with participation from Sapphire Ventures, Dragonfly, Bessemer Venture Partners, Galaxy Ventures (GLXY), FirstMark, Lightspeed (LSPD), Norwest, and Endeavor Catalyst. The financing values the provider of infrastructure for stablecoin-based payments at $1.95B, up more than 17x from its series A funding 10 months ago.

The funding round comes just four months after its series B, and brings its total capital raise to $338M. Rain will use the new funding to expand its presence in key licensed markets across North America, South America, Europe, Asia, and Africa. It will also enable Rain to deepen its full-stack stablecoin payments platform, including through strategic acquisitions, and to invest in new products that make stablecoin-powered payments feel invisible to businesses and consumers, the company said.

"In the last year, our active card base has increased 30x and our annualized payment volume has increased 38x, but we're still in the early innings," said Rain CEO and co-founder Farooq Malik. "This funding lets us bring that infrastructure to new markets and help additional enterprises go live and scale quickly everywhere."

Stablecoins have evolved from a speculative part of crypto markets and is now starting to be used in payment systems. The next phase of adoption is to make tokenized money the default method for business to move funds and for consumers to get paid, save, and spend, the company said. That requires infrastructure that allows enterprises to shift to onchain payment rails while retaining the interfaces that users are comfortable with, it added.

data.title: Track this: Strava is laying the groundwork for an IPO
data.description:
Strava appears to be laying the groundwork for an IPO. Reports indicate that the San Francisco-based company is hearing pitches from Goldman Sachs (GS) and JPMorgan (JPM) to be its underwriters for a public debut. The timing of an official IPO filing is uncertain, although sources told The Information that the company has been meeting with prospective investors. Late last year, Strava CEO Michael Martin confirmed to the Financial Times that the intention was to go public at some point.

Strava is a privately held social fitness platform founded in 2009 by Mark Gainey and Michael Horvath that lets runners, cyclists, and other athletes track activities and share them with a large online community. Over the year, Strava has invested in richer mapping, AI-assisted route recommendations, points of interest, and advanced performance tools. The company has also moved deeper into coaching and training through acquisitions such as Runna (running plans) and The Breakaway (cycling training).

Notably, Strava reported having over 180 million total registered users in the company's 2025 Year in Sport report.

On a loose basis, companies that compete with Strava through hardware, apps, and sportswear include Peloton Interactive (PTON), Planet Fitness (PLNT), Apple (AAPL),  Garmin (GRMN), Nike (NKE), Adidas (ADDYY), and Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL).

data.title: uniQure gains as FDA sets up meeting on gene therapy
data.description:
• uniQure (QURE) added ~14% in the premarket on Friday after the Dutch biotech announced that the U.S. FDA has scheduled a meeting to discuss a marketing application for its gene therapy candidate, AMT-130.
  • In November, the company disclosed that its plans to submit a Biologics License Application for AMT-130 against a neurodegenerative disorder called Huntington’s disease remain uncertain, given the regulatory feedback it received following a pre-BLA meeting.
  • However, during the newly scheduled Type A meeting, the FDA is expected to discuss the BLA data package that could support a potential accelerated approval for AMT-130, uniQure (QURE) said.
  • “We look forward to a constructive discussion with the FDA as we work toward a timely resolution regarding an accelerated approval pathway for AMT-130,” CEO Matt Kapusta added.
